description: MIRROR PvP ladder (2026-07-04, WOS_BattleReports two screenshots). DEFENDER FIXED at 6,000 ([RTS]Colonel Muller: 3,000 T7 inf + 3,000 T6 marks) EVERY battle; ATTACKER (沃草泥的马: T10 inf + T6 marks 50/50, Bradley only) VARIES 3K/6K/10K/16K. Completes the 2-count identification: v9 varied both, v9b fixed the attacker, this fixes the defender -> attacker's own-count exponent is now isolated. All attacker VICTORY, defender fully wiped, attacker MARKS untouched (all attacker casualties = infantry, severe+light).

rungs.source: NEW 2026-07-04b, attacker-heavy confirmation rung; kernel pred 329 (-2.7%)

high_corner_note: 16k->343 and 20k->338 are nearly FLAT where a pure power law keeps declining (kernel: 362, 329). The observed high-attacker curve flattens ~5% above the model - a small real wobble (likely a casualty floor from the opening exchange before the wall fully forms). A 9-point refit barely moves E,K (1.4291->1.4235, 0.1308->0.1305; RMSE 0.0286->0.0280) so the kernel is KEPT as-is; 20k is predicted at -2.7%.

mixed_composition_point.note: CORRECTION 2026-07-04 (Martin): the lancers are T6 -> NO Ambusher (unlocks T7) and NO Crystal Lance (Fire-Crystal/T11+) -> this battle is DETERMINISTIC, not stochastic. I had wrongly assumed a proc from the lancer CLASS; the skill panel is ground truth - if a proc isn't in the panel it doesn't exist. So this is a CLEAN deterministic non-50/50 composition point (only composition changed vs the 50/50 ladder; no proc confound). The total-count kernel predicts a same-size 50/50 army at 506; observed mixed = 485, i.e. composition shifts casualties only ~-4%. One point, but clean: the kernel is roughly composition-robust here. Engine proc gating fixed to match (Ambusher tier>=7, Crystal Lance tier>=11).

consistency_checks: ALL PASS. Both new reports internally consistent: survivors = total - severe - light; attacker total kills = defender severe bucket (2100) every battle; defender total kills = attacker SEVERE bucket (272 @3K, 121 @16K); defender wiped (2100 severe + 3900 light = 6000). Attacker marks untouched both.
key_finding: The 3K attacker WON despite being outnumbered 2:1 by the 6K T7 defender (I had flagged this as a coin-flip risk) - the T10 tier edge carried it, so all four rungs are clean uncensored attacker-casualty points. No fallback 4K/5K rung needed.

reduced_form_fit.law: att_inf_incap = k * N_att^u * N_def^v  (N = TOTAL troops each side, composition fixed 50/50)
reduced_form_fit.quality: OLS over all 8 UNIQUE (N_att,N_def) points across v9+v9b+v9c. log-space RMSE 0.026; max abs error 4.6% (16K rung). This single law reconciles all three ladders - the controlled campaign successfully identified the reduced casualty kernel that the 8 uncontrolled whale reports could NOT.

duration_proxy.law: bradley_high_procs = m * N_att^s * N_def^t (COARSE integer proxy for turn count)
duration_proxy.note: bigger attacker -> faster wipe -> fewer procs; bigger defender -> longer battle

mechanistic_kernel_preliminary.form: each side kill-rate R = c * N_own^p * N_enemy^q (2-count: own-frontage + target-abundance)
mechanistic_kernel_preliminary.reading: NEAR-SYMMETRIC: both sides' offense is sublinear in their OWN count (~0.71, frontage-limited) with a target-abundance term (~0.25). Derived by hand from casualty-exponents (u,v) + duration-exponents (s,t); the duration side is COARSE (integer procs) so alpha/gamma/beta/delta carry ~+-0.1 uncertainty. The turn-by-turn engine fit (workflow) will pin these precisely.
mechanistic_kernel_preliminary.prior_beast_engine: beast frontage exponent was ed=0.483 (single-sided vs a beast); PvP two-army frontage is ~0.7 - a DIFFERENT regime, as expected.

confirmed_kernel.per_turn_kernel: R_side = k * N_own * N_enemy^(ed-1); linear own + target-abundance 0.571 on enemy
confirmed_kernel.validation: train log-RMSE 0.0286 (max 5.6%), leave-one-rung-out CV 0.0400 (beats OLS baseline CV 0.0434); params rock-stable across folds. Independently re-derived from scratch -> identical E,K.
confirmed_kernel.provenance: derive-pvp-kernel design-panel workflow (2026-07-04): 4 kernel families (asym_power, sym_power, saturation, beast_port) each fit + adversarially leave-one-rung-out CV. WINNER beast_port. asym/sym reduce to the SAME 2-param law (gauge-degenerate; only E and K identifiable from a full-wipe defender - the individual own/enemy exponent split touches only DURATION). saturation degenerated to ~linear-own (W huge), corroborating beast_port.
confirmed_kernel.regime: 50/50 comp only; T10-attacker-vs-T7-defender only (K bakes in the tier gap); attacker-wins-full-wipe only; no procs. HIGH confidence inside this box.
confirmed_kernel.not_unified: enemy_ab=0.571 transplanted onto r6/r8 (~1.6M troops) with the small-battle rate -> scale blowup, mutual-wipe turn 1. pvp_engine keeps its own r6/r8 calibration (enemy_ab defaults 0.0). Two regimes, not yet one kernel.

status: SOLVED for its regime. Kernel in wos_sim/pvp_kernel.py, regression-guarded (check #7, ALL GREEN). Open (non-blocking): another attacker-heavy rung for the +5.6% corner; a non-50/50 comp rung; a different tier matchup to test ed universality.
